message:
Refactor provider hot-swap into reusable helpers with API key resolution

Extract the duplicated provider-swap logic from run_to_completion and
its abort variant into apply_session_model_selection(), eliminating
~50 lines of duplication. Add resolve_stream_api_key_for_model() that
checks AuthStorage first, then falls back to the model entry's api_key
field — ensuring stream options get the correct API key and headers
when switching providers mid-session.

Beads issue tracker synced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1e1b","title":"Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations","description":"# Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations

## Context
The Rust extension runtime uses:
- src/extensions_js.rs: JsExtensionLoadSpec, swc TypeScript compilation, QuickJS execution
- src/extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (pattern detection), load_all_extensions(), ExtensionManager
- src/extension_dispatcher.rs: hostcall dispatch

Extensions are loaded via JsExtensionLoadSpec -> swc transpiles TS to JS -> QuickJS executes -> snapshot_extensions() captures JsExtensionSnapshot (tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers).

## What To Do
1. Write a minimal Rust test that creates a JsExtensionLoadSpec for hello.ts
2. Load it through the extension pipeline
3. Capture the resulting JsExtensionSnapshot
4. Verify the snapshot contains expected registrations
5. Serialize the snapshot as JSON for comparison

## Key Files
- src/extensions_js.rs (JsExtensionLoadSpec, load flow)
- src/extensions.rs (CompatibilityScanner, ExtensionManager)
- tests/e2e_extension_registration.rs (existing test patterns)

## Acceptance Criteria
- hello.ts loads without panics
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures expected registrations
- Snapshot can be serialized to JSON","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:56.677449417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:35:55.801256207Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:35:55.801191697Z","close_reason":"Rust QuickJS runtime validated: hello.ts and all 60 official extensions load successfully.  {"id":"bd-1elj","title":"Enable markdown feature in rich_rust for assistant response rendering","description":"Enable the markdown feature in rich_rust to properly render assistant responses with formatting.

## Current State
- rich_rust has features = ["full"] but markdown rendering not used
- Assistant responses with markdown (headers, bold, lists, code blocks) render as plain text
- glamour is used for markdown in interactive TUI but not for print mode

## Implementation
1. Ensure 'markdown' feature is enabled in Cargo.toml (check if included in 'full')
2. Use rich_rust::Markdown in print mode output (src/main.rs, src/tui.rs)
3. Ensure PiConsole has method to render markdown content
4. Test with various markdown constructs

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~100KB (pulldown-cmark dependency)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: markdown rendering produces expected ANSI output
- Manual test: pi -p 'Write a list of 3 items' shows formatted list

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (verify feature)
- src/tui.rs (add markdown rendering method)
- src/main.rs (use for print mode)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.228230583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960168833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960105465Z","close_reason":"Completed: print-mode assistant output renders Markdown via rich_rust::Markdown; unit tests added; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1elj","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.234790707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Implement microtask + promise ordering and timer scheduling per bd-123.
- Provide a hostcall bridge: JS promises resolve/reject only via the connector dispatcher (timeouts/cancel/policy errors correctly mapped).
- Load/inject required compatibility shims (Node core modules + globals) via the extc pipeline + module loader contract.
- Emit structured logs for JS task scheduling, hostcalls, policy decisions, and wasm-bridge activity.

- Deterministic ordering is testable and reproducible under harness control.

- Microtasks/job queue: QuickJS requires the embedder to drain pending jobs (Promises/queueMicrotask) via JS_ExecutePendingJob. This is the foundation of the PiJS deterministic tick model.
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Module resolution: embedder can install a custom module loader (JS_SetModuleLoaderFunc) to control how import/module names resolve (critical for node:* shims and virtual modules).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Resource limits: QuickJS supports a memory cap (JS_SetMemoryLimit) and an interrupt hook (JS_SetInterruptHandler) suitable for CPU/time budgets (terminate runaway extensions deterministically).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h
  Ref (overview + timeout mention): https://quickjs-ng.github.io/quickjs-ng/developer-guide/intro.html

- rquickjs maps these controls into Rust-level APIs (Runtime::set_memory_limit, set_max_stack_size, set_gc_threshold, etc.).
  Ref: https://docs.rs/rquickjs/latest/rquickjs/struct.Runtime.html
